changelog:
Comprehensive review pass — limits actually limit now, /v1/responses tracked.

Gaps fixed (silent breakage from earlier phases):

1. Per-key limits had no real teeth. ConsumeAPIKey returned (rejected, reason) but every call site discarded the return value with `_, _ =`. After a key hit its limit, requests still succeeded and the counter just stopped moving — limits failed open. Added config.CheckAPIKeyLimit (read-only check, no commit) and a new handler.enforceAPIKeyLimit gate that runs at the start of every authenticated request. Exhausted keys now get HTTP 429 BEFORE any upstream Kiro account quota is burned.

2. /v1/responses (HTTP SSE) and the WebSocket transport were ignoring per-key tracking entirely. Both passed apiKeyID="" to recordSuccess, so Codex CLI traffic accumulated zero per-key statistics. Wired apiKeyID through both paths: pre-flight gate, ConsumeAPIKey on success, real apiKeyID in recordSuccess/recordFailure.

3. Removed dead helpers: preflightAPIKey (had a TODO admitting a known counter-overcount bug, never called) and consumeMatchedAPIKey (never called). Replaced with the simpler matchedAPIKeyID + enforceAPIKeyLimit pair.

What was solid (verified, no change):
- ConsumeAPIKey 8-step ordered checks correct
- Save() does not re-acquire cfgLock so no deadlock from Load()/ConsumeAPIKey calling it under the write lock
- minuteBucketKey/hourBucketKey unexported (don't persist) — first call after restart resets MinuteRequests/HourRequests via the bucket-roll branch, no stale-counter bug
- WebSocket auth via Authorization header works for Codex (CLI sets it on upgrade)
- All 7 success/failure paths reach stats.Record via recordSuccess/recordFailure

A15: validateApiKey legacy fast-path tracking gap + datetime-local roundtrip.
A14: persistent SQLite statistics (forever).
A13: highly customizable api keys.
A12: unify api keys.
A11: WebSocket transport.
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
